description_long: This is a traditional pitchfork featuring four long, evenly spaced metal tines that are sharply pointed for efficient piercing and lifting. The tines are attached to a robust, dark metal socket that transitions into a straight, cylindrical wooden handle. The handle is smooth and appears to be made from a single piece of wood, with a natural brown finish that contrasts with the blackened metal of the tines and socket. The overall construction suggests durability and utility, suitable for tasks such as moving hay, leaves, or debris in urban gardens or parks. The metal tines are slightly tapered and uniformly aligned, providing a balanced and symmetrical appearance. There are no visible signs of rust, wear, or additional grip enhancements on the handle.
description: A classic four-tined pitchfork with a dark metal head and a straight wooden handle, designed for lifting or moving materials. The tines are sharp and evenly spaced, and the handle is smooth and unadorned.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four sharp, evenly spaced metal tines and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the tines and handle, with the tines appearing as a single line.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with all four tines and the handle visible, showing the same features as the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile of the tines and handle, similar to view 1.
